subject: Mathematics
chapter_name: Sides and Angles of a Triangle

note_data.title: Sides and Angles of a Triangle
keyPoints: Triangle Inequality — The sum of any two sides of a triangle is greater than the third side
Angle Sum Property — The sum of all interior angles of a triangle is 180°
Exterior Angle Property — An exterior angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of two non-adjacent interior angles
In any triangle, the greater side is opposite to the greater angle and vice versa
In any triangle, the smaller side is opposite to the smaller angle and vice versa
The perpendicular from any vertex to the opposite side is the shortest distance from the vertex to that side
If two sides of a triangle are equal, then the angles opposite to them are also equal (Isosceles triangle property)
The sum of any two sides of a triangle is greater than the third side, and the difference of any two sides is less than the third side

definitions.definition: In any triangle, the sum of any two sides is greater than the third side
An angle formed by one side of a triangle and the extension of an adjacent side
An angle formed by two sides of a triangle inside the triangle
A triangle in which two sides are equal and the angles opposite to equal sides are also equal
A triangle in which all three sides are of different lengths and all three angles are of different measures

importantQuestions.question: State the triangle inequality theorem.
importantQuestions.answer: Triangle Inequality Theorem states that in any triangle, the sum of any two sides is greater than the third side. If a, b, c are sides of triangle then a + b > c, b + c > a, and a + c > b.

importantQuestions.question: Prove that an exterior angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of two non-adjacent interior angles.
importantQuestions.answer: Given: Triangle ABC with exterior angle ACD. To Prove: ∠ACD = ∠A + ∠B. Proof: In triangle ABC, ∠A + ∠B + ∠ACB = 180° (angle sum property). Also, ∠ACB + ∠ACD = 180° (linear pair). Therefore, ∠A + ∠B + ∠ACB = ∠ACB + ∠ACD. Subtracting ∠ACB from both sides: ∠A + ∠B = ∠ACD. Hence proved.

commonMistakes: Students often forget to check triangle inequality when given three sides
Confusing interior and exterior angles in theorem statements
Not drawing proper diagrams with correct labeling of angles and sides
Writing angle sum as 360° instead of 180° for triangles
Forgetting to mention 'non-adjacent' when stating exterior angle theorem
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and 1 long proof question in every BSEK paper
Always draw and label diagrams properly - marks are awarded for correct diagrams
Memorize the exact statement of triangle inequality theorem
Practice proof of exterior angle theorem with proper steps and reasons
Learn to identify which angle is greater when sides are given
formulas: Triangle Inequality: a + b > c, b + c > a, a + c > b
Exterior Angle = Sum of two non-adjacent interior angles
note_data.keyDiagram: Triangle ABC with exterior angle ACD formed by extending side BC. Label all interior angles A, B, ACB and exterior angle ACD. This diagram is essential for proving exterior angle theorem and appears in every BSEK exam.
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q3 (4 marks) — asked students to state and prove the angle sum property of a triangle with proper diagram
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q7 (6 marks) — required proof of exterior angle theorem and its application to find unknown angles
BSEK 2024 SSC-II Q4 (5 marks) — asked to define isosceles triangle properties and prove base angles are equal
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q6 (4 marks) — tested triangle inequality theorem statement and verification with given side lengths

workedExample.question: In triangle ABC, angle A = 60°, angle B = 45°. Find angle C and state which theorem you used. (4 marks)
steps: Step 1: State the angle sum property of triangle
workedExample.answer: By angle sum property of triangle: sum of all interior angles equals 180°. Therefore ∠A + ∠B + ∠C = 180°. Substituting: 60° + 45° + ∠C = 180°, so ∠C = 75°.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for stating theorem, 1 mark for correct equation, 1 mark for substitution, 1 mark for correct answer

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by writing general answers instead of textbook-exact definitions. Always use the STBB textbook phrasing for triangle properties.
Many students forget to draw proper diagrams with correct labeling when proving triangle theorems - diagrams carry 1-2 marks in proofs.
Common error: confusing exterior angle theorem with angle sum property. Exterior angle equals sum of two non-adjacent interior angles, not all three angles.
note_data.quickRecap: Triangle angle sum = 180° always. Exterior angle equals sum of two non-adjacent interior angles. In isosceles triangle, base angles are equal. Triangle inequality: sum of any two sides must be greater than third side.
importantFormulas: Exterior angle = Sum of two non-adjacent interior angles
Triangle inequality: a + b > c, b + c > a, a + c > b
